data.title: Hecla Mining sells Casa Berardi mine in Quebec to Orezone Gold for up to $593M
data.description:
Hecla Mining (HL) said Monday it agreed to sell the Casa Berardi operation in Quebec to Orezone Gold (ORZCF) for as much as $593M in cash and stock, which the company said advances its transformation to focus on its premier silver assets.

Under the deal terms, Hecla (HL) said it expects to receive $160M in cash upon closing, $80M in deferred cash, equity consideration of ~65.7M Orezone (ORZCF) common shares currently valued at ~$112M, and contingent consideration of up to $241M.

Earlier, Hecla (HL) said FY 2025 silver production reached 17M oz, up more than 5% Y/Y and at the top end of guidance, and gold output totaled 150,509 oz, exceeding both the previous year's 141,923 oz and the top end of guidance.

For FY 2026, Hecla (HL) guided for consolidated silver production of 15.1M-16.5M oz and gold production of 134K-146K oz, expecting weaker ore grades at key mines.

The company said it expects to maintain strong silver margins in 2026, with guidance all-in sustaining costs of $15.00-$16.25/oz, with potential to beat cost guidance if current prices persist as guidance is based off by-product price assumptions below current spot prices.

Hecla (HL) +1.5%, off earlier highs, as gold extended its rally, powering past $5,100/oz for the first time.

data.title: Lazard, Evercore get new Buys, Piper Sandler lands at Underperform: BofA
data.description:
BofA Securities on Monday started coverage on boutique investment banks Evercore (EVR) and Lazard (LAZ) with Buy ratings, reflecting the expectation for record dealmaking activity and other positive macro developments.

"Our positive stance reflects expectations for record M&A activity driven by pent up demand among both strategics and financial sponsors, a more accommodative regulatory environment, stable to declining interest rates, and ample credit availability," analyst Ebrahim Poonawala wrote in a note.

Still, the analyst initiated Piper Sandler (PIPR) stock at Underperform, albeit with a constructive view, "driven by the firm’s limited leverage to large cap strategic and sponsor led transactions, particularly in technology, which we expect to be central to the next leg of the M&A recovery," Poonawala noted.

In afternoon trading, Evercore (EVR) shares edged up 0.5%, Lazard (LAZ) +2% and Piper (PIPR) -1.5%.

Seeking Alpha's peer comparison page assesses various metrics for the three stocks.

data.title: GameStop pops as Michael Burry is a buyer of Cohen’s cash-rich strategy
data.description:
Investor Michael Burry has disclosed a long position in GameStop (GME), highlighting what he sees as the company’s untapped value and strategic optionality with CEO Ryan Cohen at the helm.

Writing on his Substack, Burry framed GameStop not as a typical retail business, but as a cash-generating platform with a young, disciplined leadership team and the optionality to deploy billions in a patient, intelligent manner. 

Burry’s analysis underscores the ongoing transformation under Cohen, who has been strategically reducing store count, divesting non-core assets, and leveraging warrants and 0% convertible debt to raise capital. These moves have fortified GameStop’s balance sheet, now boasting roughly $8.8 billion in cash and $4.4 billion in interest-free debt, while sustaining tangible book value growth amid a shrinking operating business. 

Burry also examines past short squeezes and the company’s approach to net operating losses, which can shelter future income. While Burry expresses skepticism about another “mother of all short squeezes,” he views Cohen’s measured capital allocation and potential acquisitions as key drivers of shareholder value.

“The value is not in another big short squeeze,” Burry wrote, but in the optionality of billions in the hands of a capable and patient management team.

“I own GME. I have been buying recently. I expect I am buying at what may soon be 1x tangible book value / 1x net asset value,” Burry added.

On Monday, GME pushed up 8.5% as it now finds itself higher on the 2026 trading year by 23.5%.

data.title: Baker Hughes projects $3B in data center orders by 2027 as power systems drive growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Baker Hughes (BKR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lorenzo Simonelli highlighted a strong fourth quarter and full year, noting, "Adjusted EBITDA totaled $1.34 billion, surpassing the midpoint of our guidance range and contributing to a record full year adjusted EBITDA of $4.83 billion." Simonelli emphasized record performance from industrial and energy technology, offsetting oilfield services softness. He detailed exceptional order momentum in power systems, LNG, and new energy, pointing to IET's record backlog of $32.4 billion and free cash flow of $2.7 billion. Key strategic developments included the scaling of the NovaLT industrial gas turbines, a $1 billion slot reservation for data center applications, and a major BRUSH generators contract for U.S. utility-scale plants. Simonelli stated, "We are targeting $2.4 billion to $2.6 billion of new energy orders in 2026."
  • CFO Ahmed Moghal reported, "Adjusted EBITDA of $1.34 billion increased by 2% year-over-year, driven by continued IET growth while OFSE results were impacted by macro-driven headwinds." Moghal confirmed $7.9 billion in total company orders and a balance sheet with $3.7 billion in cash. He noted the completion of divestitures generating $1.5 billion in cash and outlined progress on Chart integration planning, with an expected closing in Q2 2026. Moghal added, "We are making progress on our comprehensive evaluation...executing incremental targeted cost-out initiatives with quick cash backs that are expected to support durable margin expansion as we move through 2026."

Outlook

  • Management projects company revenue of $27.25 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $4.85 billion for 2026. Free cash flow conversion is expected to approach 50%. IET orders are guided in the $13.5 billion to $15.5 billion range for 2026, aiming for a fourth consecutive year at or above $13 billion. Simonelli reiterated confidence in the $40 billion, three-year IET orders target. IET revenue is expected at $13.5 billion with EBITDA of $2.7 billion, targeting a 20% margin. OFSE revenue is projected at $13.75 billion with EBITDA of $2.475 billion; organic margins are forecast to be flat year-over-year. First quarter 2026 guidance includes total company revenues of $6.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $1.06 billion.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA reached $1.34 billion, with adjusted earnings per share (EPS) at $0.78. GAAP diluted EPS was $0.88. Free cash flow for the quarter was $1.34 billion. IET revenue for the quarter was $3.81 billion, exceeding guidance and delivering segment EBITDA of $761 million with a 20% margin. OFSE delivered $3.57 billion in revenue and $647 million in EBITDA, with margins at 18.1%. For the year, IET revenue grew 10% to $13.4 billion, and EBITDA rose 21% to $2.5 billion.

Q&A

  • Arun Jayaram, JPMorgan: Asked about Power Systems strategy and sustaining growth. Ahmed Moghal outlined, "We believe that we're in a global power demand multiyear growth cycle...In 2025, Power Systems orders totaled $2.5 billion with $1 billion directly linked to data center applications...we now see data center orders to total $3 billion between 2025 and 2027."
  • Scott Gruber, Citigroup: Inquired about the $14.5 billion IET order guide for 2026 and growth drivers. CEO Simonelli responded, "Looking at 2026...it's going to be the aspect of strong pipelines in Power Systems...Gas infrastructure, as you continue to see the growth in gas...new energy, we set a record in 2025 with $2 billion in orders and expect this trajectory to continue."
  • Saurabh Pant, BofA: Asked about margin resilience and cost actions. CFO Moghal explained, "Achieving 20% margin in 2026 would represent about 150 basis points year-over-year increase...conversion of our higher-margin Gas Tech Equipment backlog...growth in Gas Tech Services...and Cordant." He also detailed flat organic margins for OFSE despite revenue declines.
  • James West, Melius: Sought update on the comprehensive strategic evaluation. Simonelli reiterated, "Our immediate focus is on completing the pending Chart acquisition and subsequently ensuring a disciplined value creative accretive integration."
  • Marc Bianchi, TD Cowen: Asked about opportunities in Venezuela. Simonelli cited historical context, ongoing presence, and potential revenue upside, while stressing a "prudent long-term view" and focus on employee safety and legal clarity.
  • John Anderson, Barclays: Queried NovaLT capacity and data center slot reservations. CFO Moghal stated, "We are on track to double our Nova capacity by the first half of '27...our Nova slots are effectively full through 2028."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on strategic growth in Power Systems and data centers, with questions signaling positive interest in order visibility and market expansion. The tone was largely constructive, pressing for specifics on margin durability, order pipelines, and capacity.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently emphasizing terms like "we are confident," "excited," and "multiyear cycle." The tone was consistently optimistic, especially regarding data center opportunities and the Chart acquisition. Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appeared incrementally more positive, particularly on Power Systems and margin expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 reinforced mid-single-digit organic EBITDA growth and margin expansion, consistent with prior outlooks but now with a clearer focus on data center-driven Power Systems growth. The previous quarter's emphasis on LNG and gas infrastructure persisted, but this call featured heightened discussion of data center demand, NovaLT orders, and BRUSH generator contracts. IET's backlog and order targets were restated and expanded upon, while management reiterated cost discipline and integration progress with the Chart acquisition. Analyst questions in both quarters targeted growth engines and margin drivers, but this quarter reflected increased conviction in the scale and durability of Power Systems.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing geopolitical and trade-related uncertainty, macro-driven headwinds in oilfield services, and tariff-related cost pressures. Simonelli described oil prices as "somewhat volatile in recent months" and projected low single-digit declines in global upstream spending for 2026. The company is monitoring regulatory reviews for the Chart acquisition and highlighted the importance of integration and cost synergy capture. In Venezuela, Simonelli emphasized the need for a prudent approach and legal clarity before expanding activity. CFO Moghal noted ongoing productivity and cost optimization efforts to offset market and mix headwinds, as well as the impact of recent divestitures.

Final Takeaway

Baker Hughes delivered a record year in 2025, with strong order momentum and margin expansion across Power Systems, LNG, and new energy, while maintaining disciplined execution amid market headwinds. Management projects continued growth in 2026, targeting $3 billion in data center-related orders by 2027 and sustained margin improvement through cost initiatives and the integration of Chart. The company remains committed to delivering durable free cash flow, operational resilience, and long-term value to shareholders as it expands its industrial energy solutions portfolio.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Boeing Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Boeing (BA) is set to post fourth quarter results on Tuesday, before markets close.

Wall Street expects the Virginia-based company to post EPS of -$0.39 on revenue of $22.84 billion, implying a rise of nearly 50% during the quarter.

The plane maker, in October, posted a wider-than-expected quarterly loss and confirmed another delay for its 777X widebody jet, which is now expected to reach customers in 2027. However, analysts said the industrial and defense giant has made progress in stabilizing production on its 737 and 787 programs.

UBS analysis that tracks Dreamlifter cargo flights as a leading indicator of aircraft assembly activity in January said Boeing’s 787 production appears to be gaining momentum after a seasonal holiday slowdown.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish and rated the stock a Buy.

“With the Spirit AeroSystems acquisition completed and production recovering, investor focus is expected to shift toward 777X certification and the pace of free cash flow improvement over the next several years,” said Susquehanna, adding that U.S. aerospace and defense companies are entering the fourth quarter earnings season with favorable fundamentals across commercial aviation, defense, and aftermarket services.

Despite the improvement, Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider the stock a Hold.

“BA’s defense and services segments show margin improvement and stability, yet commercial aircraft remains a significant risk due to execution challenges and contract legacy issues,” argued Seeking Alpha analyst Kenio Fontes.

Over the last two years, Boeing has beaten both revenue and EPS estimates 50% of the time. Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen one upward revision, compared to15 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have been revised upwards eight times versus12 downward moves.

The stock has grown over 26% last year, outperforming the nearly 17% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: BlackRock TCP Capital stock plunges after loan writedowns
data.description:
BlackRock TCP Capital (TCPC) shares plunged 15% in Monday afternoon trading after the business development company disclosed writedowns on troubled portfolio investments.

The BlackRock (BLK) middle-market debt fund has said the writedowns were expected to reduce net value per share by about 19% as of the end of 2025, according to preliminary estimates in a recent U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing. That would imply a decline to $7.06 from $8.71 at the end of Q3.

Six investments accounted for about two-thirds of the NAV decline, the fund said. Meanwhile, BlackRock TCP (TCPC) noted it has waived one-third of its management fee for Q4.

Net investment income for the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025, is anticipated to be  $0.24-$0.26 (midpoint $0.25), trailing the $0.27 consensus.

data.title: Noble jumps after winning $1.3B in new drilling contracts, enters Norway floater market
data.description:
Noble Corp. (NE) +4.6% in Monday's trading after announcing new contract awards for nine rigs totaling ~$1.3B of backlog, including a three-year deal that marks the company's entry into Norway’s harsh-environment floater market.

The largest award is a three-year contract for the previously idle Noble GreatWhite semisubmersible with Aker BP for offshore Norway, expected to begin in Q2 2027, representing ~$473M in total value; it marks the rig's first campaign in Norway and a significant step in expanding the company's presence on the Norwegian Continental Shelf.

Noble (NE) also said the Noble Gerry de Souza drillship was awarded a two-year contract by Exxon Mobil (XOM) in Nigeria, with options for up to three additional years; operations are targeted for mid-2026 and the contract is expected to add $292M in backlog.

In Guyana, Exxon (XOM) awarded two additional rig years under an existing commercial enabling agreement, extending contracts evenly across four drillships - Noble Sam Croft, Noble Don Taylor, Noble Tom Madden, and Noble Bob Douglas - through February 2029.

The company also said the Noble Endeavor semi-submersibile was awarded an 11-well contract with an undisclosed operator in South America, expected to begin in late 2026 at a $300K dayrate with the potential for additional revenue from a performance incentive provision, and the Noble Developer semi-submersible was awarded a three-well contract with estimated duration of 240 days with BP in Trinidad, scheduled to begin in Q1 2027 at a $375K dayrate with options for up to three additional wells with estimated combined duration of 240 days.

Also, the Noble BlackRhino drillship was awarded a contract for one workover well with Beacon Offshore Energy in the U.S. Gulf, scheduled to begin in March 2026 with an estimated duration of 50 days.

"The redeployment of four currently idle deepwater rigs should drive a meaningful utilization improvement across our fleet, with 92% of our 24 marketed floaters now contracted compared to 75% in our prior fleet status report," Noble (NE) President and CEO Robert Eifler said.

data.title: Zoom jumps as Baird highlights Anthropic stake
data.description:
• Zoom Communications (ZM) shares jumped more than 10% on Monday as investment firm Baird highlighted its stake in generative artificial intelligence darling Anthropic (ANTHRO).
  • “While we (and the market) have been primarily focused on ZM's ability to gradually reaccelerate revenue growth and capture AI opportunities, the quieter, hidden gem might be its $51 million investment in Anthropic,” Baird analyst William Power wrote in a note to clients. Power reiterated his Outperform rating and $95 price target on Zoom.
  • Zoom invested in Anthropic, which makes the popular Claude family of AI models, in 2023, via its venture capital arm. 
  • Earlier this month, it was reported that Anthropic is in discussions about raising $10B at a $350B valuation. The company recently surpassed $9B in annual revenue run rate, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Morgan Stanley’s 10 predictions for investing in 2026
data.description:
Morgan Stanley outlined 10 investment predictions designed to help investors navigate what the firm describes as periods of extreme market volatility.

Stephen C. Byrd, equity strategist at Morgan Stanley, centered the analysis in four key themes: artificial intelligence/tech diffusion, the future of energy, the “multipolar world,” and societal shifts.

According to Byrd, societal shifts encompass the ripple effects of AI-driven employment disruption, aging populations, changing consumer preferences, the pursuit of healthy longevity, and challenging demographics across multiple geographies.

1. “Two worlds” of LLM progress and AI adoption: American frontier large language models will achieve a step-change increase in capabilities in the first half of 2026 that Chinese competitors will not match within the same timeframe. While concerns about AI adoption rates may dominate early in the year, Morgan Stanley expects bullish sentiment to emerge by the second half as non-linear increases in AI capabilities deliver widespread benefits.

2. Compute demand exceeds supply: The proliferation of AI adoption and increasing complexity of AI use cases will drive exponential net demand growth for compute resources. Despite this surge, supply growth will fall short, though Morgan Stanley’s new “Intelligence Factory” model suggests attractive economics for LLM developers at the data center level.

3. A robust U.S. policy agenda: The Trump administration is expected to take more robust action than anticipated on multiple fronts, including securing domestic supplies of critical minerals, uranium, and metals. Additional priorities include supporting the re-shoring of manufacturing, increasing military spending with an emphasis on innovation, and reducing consumer costs.

4. AI tech transfer and national self-sufficiency: In response to American advancements in AI, policy, and compute capabilities, China will exert pressure on the U.S. to permit more extensive AI technology transfer. Morgan Stanley warns that inequalities in AI capabilities at the national level can significantly impact global trade dynamics and the push for “gross domestic intelligence.”

5. The politics of energy: Rising energy costs around the world will trigger a backlash against data center growth, prompting policy support for low-cost energy solutions. Expect to see increased development of “off-grid” power supply strategies for data center projects as companies and governments seek alternatives.

6. Greater convergence of AI and energy: Key AI players will take steps to gain greater control of energy infrastructure to secure the lowest-cost, most reliable power as quickly as possible. This strategy allows them to control their destiny, insulate other power customers from AI-driven impacts, and leverage AI to drive energy efficiencies.

7. China grows tech manufacturing market share while the U.S. experiences a “reshoring renaissance”: China will deploy its considerable capabilities to expand global manufacturing market share in key tech-heavy industries. Meanwhile, structural tech diffusion will reduce the advantage of low-cost labor, tilting scales back toward domestic U.S. production, while regions with high costs, heavy regulation, and limited AI access lose ground.

8. In Latin America, a trifecta of change: policy shifts, changing geopolitics, and peak interest rates will drive Latin America toward a new investment cycle. Morgan Stanley sees a bull case for the region, driven by investment rather than consumption as the primary economic engine.

9. Re-skilling initiatives and AI job loss policy intervention: Corporations and governments will launch wide-ranging initiatives to re-skill workers in response to AI-driven job shifts. Political sensitivity to real or perceived job losses from AI adoption will result in various policy interventions aimed at managing the workforce transition.

10. “Transformative AI” drives deflation and economic shifts: The second half of 2026 will reveal early signs of rapid price declines across a range of economic activity driven by transformative AI capabilities. Morgan Stanley expects these deflationary pressures to contribute to greater wage inequality, higher capital expenditures, potential upward pressure on interest rates, and rising valuations for assets that cannot be replicated by AI.

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

Green Energy: (ICLN), (TAN), (FAN), (GRID), (QCLN), and (ERTH).

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI).

Cloud Computing ETFs: (SKYY), (WCLD), (CLOU), (FCLD), (SKYU), and (CLOD).

data.title: Wall Street rises as investors focus on mega-cap earnings, gold rally
data.description:
U.S. stocks were higher on Monday as mega-cap earnings took center stage, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) continued to rally beyond $5,000, and rising political uncertainty, including sharply increased odds of a U.S. government shutdown  and fresh tariff threats, persisted.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.6% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.8%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%.

Gold’s spot price (XAUUS:CUR) topped the $5,000 per ounce level for the first time on Sunday as geopolitical concerns rose. Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) also hit a record, now over $110 per ounce, as fears of a U.S. government shutdown rise, according to prediction markets.

“About a year ago, I stated that I felt [gold would] hit this milestone by the end of 2025—so we are a month behind where I thought we would be. Next stop? We are likely to see $6,000 in 2026, though I expect the climb to be a lot more volatile from here for the reasons cited in earlier posts,” said Mohamed A. El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz.

In addition, USA Rare Earth (USAR) rallied +15.6% on Monday after it entered into a non-binding LOI with the U.S. Department of Commerce and a collaboration with the U.S. Department of Energy, as the U.S. government took an equity stake.

On the earnings front, tech mega-caps such as Microsoft (MSFT), Tesla (TSLA), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL) are expected to report this week, as well as Visa (V), American Express (AXP), Chevron (CVX), and Exxon Mobil (XOM).

“S&P 500 ((SP500)) companies with more than half of their revenue coming from outside the U.S. are expected to see earnings grow 12.6% in Q4 vs. only 5.5% if more than half comes from the U.S. Further proof the global economy is doing much better than most are telling us?” said Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group.

In economic news, durable goods orders jumped 5.3% MoM in November, passing consensus. Excluding transportation, new orders also increased 0.5%, stronger than consensus, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

U.S. Treasury yields were mostly flat. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.22%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.61%.
